findings.title: Funding-block claims can be replayed and reorder payouts because the round snapshot is mutable within the same block

best_agent_match.title: Round-start claims can be replayed or reordered in the funded block to overmint rewards

findings.title: Matured stake-removal actions remain executable during a pending-claim window, letting providers claim against stake they already removed

best_agent_match.title: Pre-scheduled stake removals can be executed during a pending claim window to steal or inflate rewards

findings.title: Dust-stake accounts can fill every governance proposal slot and repeatedly delay protocol actions

best_agent_match.title: Any dust staker can monopolize all proposal slots and repeatedly stall governance

findings.title: A guardian proposal submitted when total stake is zero can become permanently unevaluable and block new proposals

best_agent_match.title: A guardian proposal submitted when total stake is zero can become permanently unevaluable

findings.title: Governance proposal code-hash pinning does not detect proxy implementation upgrades

best_agent_match.title: Governance's target-code hash check does not detect proxy implementation upgrades

findings.title: Claims use the mutable global `fundingAmount` instead of the round's snapshotted `fundedAmount`

best_agent_match.title: Claims use the mutable global `fundingAmount` instead of the round's snapshotted `fundedAmount`

findings.title: Permissionless `claimRewards` can burn a provider's entire round by finalizing a zero-value claim during temporary ineligibility

best_agent_match.title: Pre-scheduled stake removals can be executed during a pending claim window to steal or inflate rewards

rejected_candidates.title: DelegateManager.claimRewards allows unauthorized reward claims
rejected_candidates.reason: Caller control is missing, but the function does not redirect rewards to the caller; rewards are credited to the specified service provider and delegators. Reconstructed separately as a grief-only zero-value claim issue, not theft.

rejected_candidates.title: Guardian can execute arbitrary transactions without voting
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an explicit guardian privilege in the protocol design, not a bug in the implemented access control.

rejected_candidates.title: Initializable initializer modifier blocks contract initialization
rejected_candidates.reason: The initializer checks `proxyAdmin` in proxy storage; this code alone does not show initialization is impossible, and the proxy pattern is intended to supply that slot.

rejected_candidates.title: Registry has single point of failure with onlyOwner access
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a governance/trust-model centralization concern rather than a distinct implementation vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: ServiceProviderFactory _validateBalanceInternal missing deployerStake validation
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as stated: registration validates current deployer stake against the minimum, and `decreaseStake()` revalidates after updating `deployerStake`, so the claimed bypass is not supported.

rejected_candidates.title: ClaimsManager processClaim potential division by zero
rejected_candidates.reason: Too speculative as a standalone reportable issue here; reaching `totalStakedAt(fundedBlock) == 0` for a claimable provider depends on zero-minimum service-type configuration, and no distinct persistent harmful state beyond that edge case is demonstrated.

rejected_candidates.title: ServiceProviderFactory allows registration with zero stake
rejected_candidates.reason: Not unconditional: `register()` immediately calls stake-balance validation, so zero-stake registration only succeeds if governance created a service type with `minStake == 0`.

rejected_candidates.title: Governance guardian can transfer guardianship to zero address
rejected_candidates.reason: This is trusted-role self-misconfiguration, and the guardian already holds stronger powers such as veto and arbitrary guardian execution; it is not a distinct adversarial vulnerability.
